{
  "cells": [
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {},
      "source": [
        "---\n",
        "title: \"Sim Hub Template\"\n",
        "subtitle: \"Design: Two Arm + Interim, Endpoint = Binary\"\n",
        "format:\n",
        "  html:\n",
        "    embed-resources: true\n",
        "editor: source\n",
        "---\n",
        "\n",
        "Welcome to the Sim Hub Template!  This template refers to this repository which you have cloned to Copernicus.  This repository is used to simulate single designs, simulate across many scenarios, and summarize the results all in Copernicus.  \n",
        "\n",
        "If you are new to Sim Hub templates, there are a few components to be familiar with:\n",
        "\n",
        "1. `simhub-template.html` - congrats, you are here!\n",
        "2. `R/` - files to edit to customize the simulated design\n",
        "3. `_targets.R` - script to edit to modify the simulation execution on Copernicus\n",
        "\n",
        "These components are fully customizable and can be suited to simulate almost all trial designs in which patient level simulation is needed.  To understand the basic design in this template, please review the sections below and the CRAN vignettes.\n",
        "\n",
        "## Prerequisite\n",
        "\n",
        "This template assumes prior familiarity with the R package `TSLite` a lite version of the `TrialSimulator` package focused setting up patient-level simulation.. In particular, users are expected to understand the member functions for defining endpoints, arms, trial, and milestones provided by the package. To install the package, run\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r}\n",
        "#| eval: false\n",
        "#| echo: true\n",
        "if(!require(\"remotes\")){\n",
        "  install.packages(\"remotes\")\n",
        "}\n",
        "remotes::install_github(\n",
        "  \"https://github.com/Boehringer-Ingelheim/TSLite\", \n",
        "  build_manual = TRUE,\n",
        "  build_vignettes = TRUE,\n",
        "  force = TRUE\n",
        ")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "Before using this template, we recommend reviewing the following vignettes from `TrialSimulator`:\n",
        "\n",
        "-   [Define Non-Time-to-Event Endpoints](https://zhangh12.github.io/TrialSimulator/articles/defineNonTimeToEventEndpoints.html)\n",
        "-   [Define Milestones](https://zhangh12.github.io/TrialSimulator/articles/conditionSystem.html)\n",
        "-   [Action Functions](https://zhangh12.github.io/TrialSimulator/articles/actionFunctions.html)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Environment Setup\n",
        "\n",
        "This template requires a reproducible R environment managed by the `renv` package. All necessary packages and their specific versions are recorded in the `renv.lock` file included with this template. To set up the environment, run\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r, renv}\n",
        "#| eval: false\n",
        "#| echo: true\n",
        "if(!require(\"renv\")){\n",
        "  install.packages(\"renv\")\n",
        "}\n",
        "renv::restore()\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "This will automatically install the required packages and configure your session to match the template's development environment.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r file=c('doc_tools/fill.R', 'R/set_scenarios.R')}\n",
        "#| include: false\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r, load packages}\n",
        "#| include: false\n",
        "library(tidyverse)\n",
        "library(knitr)\n",
        "library(TrialSimulator)\n",
        "library(kableExtra)\n",
        "library(TSLite)\n",
        "set.seed(12345)\n",
        "targets::tar_source()\n",
        "args <- set_scenarios()[1, , drop = FALSE]\n",
        "fill_path <- fill('R/simulate.R', args)\n",
        "knitr::read_chunk(fill_path, roxygen_comments = FALSE)\n",
        "knitr::read_chunk('R/action_functions.R')\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "# Design\n",
        "\n",
        "This illustrates an editable design.  Do not edit here as this is for illustration.  \n",
        "\n",
        "To edit the design for the simulation, edit the corresponding section in `simulate()` in `R/simulate.R`.\n",
        "\n",
        "Render this document to update with new design and feel free to use this as a document for your study design.  \n",
        "\n",
        "## Design Specifications\n",
        "\n",
        "In this template, we consider a randomized study as follows\n",
        "\n",
        "-   One placebo (or SoC) arm and a treatment arm, with a 1:1 randomization ratio.\n",
        "-   Randomize 50 patients per month for the first 6 months, then 65 patients per month until `r args$n_patients` patients are recruited.\n",
        "-   Dropout rate at any time during the study is assumed to be `r args$dropout_rate`.\n",
        "-   One binary endpoint that follows a Bernoulli distribution with\n",
        "    -   Response rate in placebo and treated arms are are `r args$rates[[1]]['pbo']` and `r args$rates[[1]]['trt']`, respectively, where a greater rate indicates increased efficacy.\n",
        "    -   Endpoint is observed at 8 weeks (readout = 8)\n",
        "-   Interim analysis is performed when `r args$interim_info`% of patients reach readout time.\n",
        "    -   Various interim rules could be assessed using the information at interim, including things like conditional power.\n",
        "    -   For this case, we will assess simple statistics like p-value and observed effect size.  \n",
        "-   Final analysis is performed when all patients reach the end of the study.\n",
        "    -   The treatment effect verse placebo is estimated and tested using logistic regression model.\n",
        "    -   The treatment effect is measured by odds ratio and risk difference.\n",
        "    -   Missing data is treated as failures this can be modified using an action function (see below)\n",
        "\n",
        "## Implementation\n",
        "\n",
        "### Define Endpoints and Arms\n",
        "\n",
        "We define endpoint for each of the two arms. Because the endpoint is a binary endpoint, the `readout` argument is needed, representing the time in which the endpoint will be observed.\n",
        "\n",
        "::: panel-tabset\n",
        "## Standard of Care\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r ep_in_pbo}\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## Treated\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r ep_in_trt}\n",
        "```\n",
        ":::\n",
        "\n",
        "Within this function - the key argument is `generator` which is any R function that can be used to generate the data.  In this case it is a base function but these could be custom functions to represent any model.  All functions in `TSLite` also support `...` arguments so custom arguments for functions to `generator` can be passed in there.  \n",
        "\n",
        "### Define A Trial\n",
        "\n",
        "To define a trial, we first need to specify how patients are recruited. This can be done by specifying `accrual_rate`, which is a data frame required by the function `StaggeredRecruiter` that defines a staggered recruitment. Two columns `end_time` and `piecewise_rate` are required. For more information, see `?TrialSimulator::StaggeredRecruiter`.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r accrual_rate}\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "We define dropout using the custom function below, where dropout time is assigned a value of `0` if dropped else a value of `Inf`.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r dropout}\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "Then we initialize a trial using `trial()`.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r trial}\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "### Define Action at Milestone\n",
        "\n",
        "We need to implement custom action function for the milestone. An action function is executed automatically when the corresponding milestone is triggered. In an action function, we recommend to call `trial$get_locked_data(milestone_name)` to get access of a data frame of the data snapshot. We can then carry out statistical analysis based on the snapshot.  In this instance we are performing a logistic regression model.\n",
        "\n",
        "In the actions, a function `save()` is used to save output during a trial. Users can refer to `?TrialSimulator::Trials` and [vignette](https://zhangh12.github.io/TrialSimulator/articles/actionFunctions.html) for more information about this function. \n",
        "\n",
        "Note that missing data are assigned to be non-responders in this function.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r action}\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "We plan only one milestone for the final analysis which will occur when all data is available at the `readout`.  The key argument for this function is `when` which is highly flexible to capture many options for analysis timing for things like interims.  See the vignettes above for more details.  \n",
        "\n",
        "```{r milestone}\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "We then register the milestone to a listener who will monitor the trial.  \n",
        "\n",
        "```{r listener}\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "### Execute a Trial\n",
        "\n",
        "To execute a trial, we call the function `run()` in the controller of the trial and listener. Here we simulate one trial, so that a plot of cumulative events are generated automatically for each of the endpoints.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r, controller}\n",
        "controller <- controller(trial, listener)\n",
        "controller$run(n = 1, plot_event = FALSE)\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "We can set `n` to a larger integer for more replicates. However, we recommend to set `plot_event = FALSE` if `n` is greater than 1.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r}\n",
        "#| echo: false\n",
        "controller$reset()\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r, controller run}\n",
        "controller$run(n = 10, plot_event = FALSE, silent = TRUE)\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "### Retrieve Output\n",
        "\n",
        "We can display information saved for each of the simulated trials by the function `get_output()`. For more information about the naming convention in the output data frame, please refer to vignette of [Action Functions](https://zhangh12.github.io/TrialSimulator/articles/actionFunctions.html).\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r, get output}\n",
        "controller$get_output() %>% \n",
        "  head() %>% \n",
        "  kable(escape = TRUE) %>% \n",
        "  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = \"striped\", \n",
        "                full_width = FALSE,\n",
        "                position = \"left\") %>%\n",
        "  scroll_box(width = \"100%\")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "In this example, `milestone_time_<final analysis>` is the time of final analysis. The columns `pval`, `OR`are recorded by calling the `save()` function in the action function.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Summarize Time of Milestones\n",
        "\n",
        "We can use the member function `TrialSimulator::summarizeMilestoneTime()` to summarize milestone time easily. It is also equipped with a generic `plot` method.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r, summarize milestone}\n",
        "summarizeMilestoneTime(controller$get_output())\n",
        "# plot(summarizeMilestoneTime(controller$get_output()))\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r}\n",
        "#| include: false\n",
        "## remove temp files\n",
        "unlink(fill_path)\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "# Run in `targets`\n",
        "\n",
        "Using `targets` for simulation is entirely optional so you can stop here as  `TSLite` has the ability to run simulations which can be incorporated in workflows in the usual way.  \n",
        "\n",
        "**However,** the benefits of `targets` integrated with Copernicus HPC may be worth the effort for large simulation projects.  \n",
        "\n",
        "Because `targets` gives us the ability to run many scenarios in parallel. *Most* of the work to set up targets is already done in the template\n",
        "\n",
        "The most important part is setting up scenarios.  `targets` takes in dataframes with a row per scenario which you can use `expand_grid()` to expand across large parameter spaces very easily.  You can also do a subset by `filter()`ing to the desired rows.\n",
        "\n",
        "## Scenarios\n",
        "\n",
        "Scenarios are features that you can easily modify to explore aspects of a trial design.  These can be trial design features, like sample size, or data generation parameters, like effect size.  In practice, this will be what you are iterating across in a large simulation.  Because this can become large, leveraging an HPC using a package like `targets` let's us fully explore the scenario space.  \n",
        "\n",
        "## Set up `targets` Scenarios\n",
        "\n",
        "Go to `set_scenarios()` in `R/set_scenarios.R`.  Let's evaluate varying sample sizes: `seq(500, 700, 25)`.  To evalute a grid of the other parameters, just add to the vectors in the `expand_grid()` below.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r targets scenarios, eval = TRUE, echo = TRUE}\n",
        "set_scenarios <- function(){\n",
        "\n",
        "  tidyr::expand_grid( #expend_grid easily lets us explore the grid of scenarios across many features\n",
        "    n_patients = seq(500, 700, 10),\n",
        "    interim_info = c(0.4, 0.5, 0.75),\n",
        "    rates = list(c(pbo = 0.10, trt = 0.18)),\n",
        "    dropout_rate = c(0)\n",
        "  ) %>%\n",
        "    mutate(scenario = paste0('scenario', 1:n()))\n",
        "\n",
        "}\n",
        "\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "Let's see those scenarios - each row represents a scenario\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r view scenarios, eval = TRUE, echo = TRUE}\n",
        "set_scenarios()\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## `targets` simulations\n",
        "\n",
        "The simulation is ready to run using the template infrastructure across the scenarios.\n",
        "\n",
        "The default targets template will run on the Copernicus grid with 1k reps per scenario.  To adjust the settings of the simulation, edit `_targets.R`.\n",
        "\n",
        "**IMPORTANT: There is no need to execute any of the files that we edited**\n",
        "\n",
        "`targets` does all of this using built in `targets` functions\n",
        "\n",
        "-   `simulate()` - is the function that `targets` will iterate over\n",
        "-   `targets::tar_source()` will load `simulate()` for you to try \n",
        "-   `targets::tar_make()` - runs the simulation based on the template specification and stores them as `sims`  \n",
        "-   `targets` will automatically detect whether `sims` need to be rerun based on changes to the files\n",
        "-   `targets::tar_load(sims)` - loads the simulations to the local environment \n",
        "\n",
        "## Run your simulation!\n",
        "\n",
        "Use the code below to run the simulation.  This set to `eval = TRUE` so it will run when you rend this document.  In practice, it is better to run this directly in your console.    \n",
        "\n",
        "```{r targets make, eval = TRUE, echo = TRUE}\n",
        "#| results: 'hide'\n",
        "#| message: false\n",
        "#| warning: false\n",
        "## run simulations on grid - this will take a bit the first time that you run it\n",
        "targets::tar_make()\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## Compute OCs\n",
        "\n",
        "Let's look at the `\"Prob. Go\"` for each scenario, given that you have run the above targets simulation.\n",
        "\n",
        "You can compute and plot an such operating characteristics of interest based on your saved results from your simulation. \n",
        "\n",
        "```{r simulation summary, echo = TRUE, eval = TRUE}\n",
        "## load simulation results\n",
        "targets::tar_load(sims)\n",
        "\n",
        "## calculate power\n",
        "summary <- sims |> \n",
        "  group_by(scenario, n_patients) |>\n",
        "  summarize(power = mean(pval <= 0.025),\n",
        "  .groups = \"drop\")\n",
        "\n",
        "summary |> \n",
        "  kable(escape = TRUE) |> \n",
        "  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = \"striped\", \n",
        "                full_width = FALSE,\n",
        "                position = \"left\") |>\n",
        "  scroll_box(width = \"100%\")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "This is the end of the simulation template for this design.  Please consult with Sim Hub if you have and issues or would like help with custom modifications to any of the code above.\n"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "kernelspec": {
      "name": "python3",
      "language": "python",
      "display_name": "Python 3 (ipykernel)",
      "path": "/opt/python/3.13.5/share/jupyter/kernels/python3"
    }
  },
  "nbformat": 4,
  "nbformat_minor": 4
}
